summary: Package collects host/user identifiers and exfiltrates them over HTTPS at load
details: index.js loads `https`, `os`, and `fs` at the top level and assembles a payload of host identifiers including `os.hostname()`, `os.platform()`, the OS username, current working directory, and Node version, then transmits it via an outbound HTTPS request. The package is presented as an AWS CDK construct for SageMaker notebook workflows; collecting and shipping the installer's hostname, username, cwd, and platform fingerprint to an external endpoint is unrelated to that purpose and is a recognizable host-recon/exfiltration pattern. Behavior fires when the module is required by a CDK build/synth process, leaking developer/CI environment identifiers off-host.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
